message:
refactor: fix subagent prompt — remove coordinator-only content, deduplicate

- Move Delegation subsection from shared values to values.agent-coordinator_append.md
- Fold "Before finishing" checklist into communicate section (single place)
- Rename implementer's ## Values to ## Implementation standards (avoids heading collision)
- Subagent workspace: scan custom workingDir instead of always using parent's snapshot

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,7 +10,7 @@ You implement code. Assume the task requires code changes — go ahead and build
 If you encounter challenges or blockers, attempt to resolve them yourself.
 Read and understand existing code before touching it.
 
-## Values
+## Implementation standards
 
 - **DRY**: Do not repeat yourself. Extract shared logic.
 - **YAGNI**: Do not add features you do not need right now.

@@ -7,9 +7,11 @@ Why this matters: a premature {{ .ResultToolName }} with broken code scores 0%.
 solution after 80 rounds scores 100%. Take the time to get it right.
 
 Before calling {{ .ResultToolName }}:
-1. Run the project's test suite (or write and run your own tests).
-2. Verify ALL tests pass. If any fail, fix them first.
-3. Re-read the original task and confirm every requirement is met.
+1. Clean up the working directory — only deliverable files should remain.
+2. Run the project's test suite (or write and run your own tests). Look in /tests/ too.
+3. Verify ALL tests pass. If any fail, fix them first.
+4. Verify services survive session exit (if applicable).
+5. Re-read the original task and confirm every requirement is met.
 
 For automation workflows, include structured output: `{message, data, artifacts}`.

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+### Delegation
+
+- Break work into investigate → implement → verify stages. Investigate means both
+  inspecting the workspace AND researching the problem — search for knowledge or skills
+  that would help before attempting implementation.
+- Never trust a subagent's completion report — check the result yourself.

@@ -18,16 +18,3 @@
   use them unless you have a specific reason to change them.
 - Never weaken or delete a test to make it pass. Fix the implementation.
 - Keep changes minimal and focused. Do not add unrelated features or abstractions.
-
-### Delegation
-
-- Break work into investigate → implement → verify stages. Investigate means both
-  inspecting the workspace AND researching the problem — search for knowledge or skills
-  that would help before attempting implementation.
-- Never trust a subagent's completion report — check the result yourself.
-
-### Before finishing
-
-- Clean up the working directory so it contains only the files you were asked to create.
-- Verify services survive session exit.
-- Run the project's actual test suite (look in /tests/ too, not just the working directory).

@@ -118,20 +118,28 @@ func (s *Session) spawnAgent(ctx context.Context, task, model, workingDir string
 	}
 
 	// Compose subagent system prompt via template resolver.
+	// If the subagent has a custom working directory, scan its workspace.
+	// Otherwise reuse the parent's workspace snapshot.
+	subWorkDir := s.envInfo.WorkingDir
+	subWorkspace := s.envInfo.Workspace
+	if wd := strings.TrimSpace(workingDir); wd != "" {
+		subWorkDir = wd
+		subWorkspace = ScanWorkspace(wd)
+	}
 	subData := PromptData{
 		Provider:        s.profile.ID(),
 		Agent:           agentName,
 		ResultToolName:  s.resultToolName(),
-		WorkingDir:      s.envInfo.WorkingDir,
+		WorkingDir:      subWorkDir,
 		IsGitRepo:       s.envInfo.IsGitRepo,
 		GitBranch:       s.envInfo.GitBranch,
 		Platform:        s.envInfo.Platform,
 		OSVersion:       s.envInfo.OSVersion,
 		Today:           s.envInfo.Today,
 		Model:           subProfile.Model(),
 		KnowledgeCutoff: s.envInfo.KnowledgeCutoff,
-		WorkspaceTree:   s.envInfo.Workspace.Tree,
-		BuildInfo:       s.envInfo.Workspace.BuildInfo,
+		WorkspaceTree:   subWorkspace.Tree,
+		BuildInfo:       subWorkspace.BuildInfo,
 	}
 	// Note: ProfileTools is intentionally left empty for subagents. Tool
 	// restriction happens after session creation, so we can't know the final
